script_text: I had a client who wanted to put a lot of their portfolio in one tech stock, Mostly because they worked in that industry and felt confident about it. I recommended more diversification… based on their risk tolerance. But they pushed back,um…, because they felt they had good insight.Instead of just agreeing uh.., or arguing, I asked them to walk me through their thinking, so I could understand it better. Then we talked through some scenario…,like, for example, what would happen if that stock dropped? I acknowledged that they knew the industry well but explained diversification was really about managing risk. We ended up compromising…., they kept a bigger position than I suggested, but not as concentrated as they wanted. And, they appreciated that I listened.
